scientific_question: After restricting the benchmark to complete biological target-binder dimers, can both frozen predictors recover native complexes before generated designs or a trust gate are evaluated?

historical_boundary.historical_claim: W2b, W2c, and W3b remain exact prepared-two-chain structural-proxy experiments and do not estimate strict biological target-binder generalization.

stages.name: fresh target discovery and representation lock
stages.stop_rule: Stop unless exactly eight fresh targets pass every target-validity and overlap check.

stages.name: target-MSA preparation
stages.stop_rule: Stop unless all eight target MSAs pass sequence, depth, hash, and no-truncation checks.

stages.name: native dual-predictor recoverability screen
stages.target_pass_rule: Both predictors must produce strict-QC records with finite interface pAE and L-RMSD below 4.0 A against the native complex.
stages.stage_pass_rule: At least six of eight targets must pass the target-level native recoverability rule.
stages.stop_rule: If fewer than six targets pass, stop before candidate generation and revise representation or predictor scope.

claim_boundary.maximum_supported_native_screen_claim: The frozen predictors can or cannot recover the native complexes for the prospectively valid target set under the locked representation.

next_action: Discover and lock eight fresh strict target-binder dimers. Do not prepare MSA or H100 approval packets until the target manifest and representation audit pass exactly.
